rows.acceptance: Route-task now treats Rust route primitives, habit matching, reflex matching, and complexity classification as authoritative by default and returns `MANUAL` with explicit `route_error` when Rust outputs are unavailable/invalid; optional TS fallback is explicit via `ROUTE_TASK_TS_FALLBACK=1`. Regression and sovereignty suites stay green. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.

Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.

rows.acceptance: Implement deterministic hidden-eval execution + receipts (seeded case generation, pass/fail scores, reason codes), wire outcomes into `setAttemptOutcome`/cooldown/promotion logic, and add tests for pass/fail branches affecting candidate outcomes under shadow/live modes. rows.acceptance: Add pre-admission state-delta checks and semantic dedupe so unchanged proposals are parked before attempt accounting, with explicit reason receipts and configurable freshness windows. Verify with 30-day replay and hold-taxonomy receipts; success criteria: reduce unchanged-state holds by >=50% while preserving shipped yield. Rollback by disabling admission delta gate and reverting to baseline queue policy profile.
rows.problem: Repeated `stop_repeat_gate_unchanged_state` holds are consuming attempt budget without producing net-new actionability.
rows.title: Unchanged-State Admission Gate

rows.acceptance: Implement calibrated confidence thresholds with a bounded canary-execute band for mid-confidence proposals, plus replay-backed precision/recall receipts and automatic rollback to prior thresholds on safety regressions. Success criteria: cut low-confidence holds by >=40% while keeping safety-stop rate within existing warn band.
rows.problem: `score_only_fallback_low_execution_confidence` holds are blocking potentially actionable work because routing thresholds are too conservative for current signal quality.
rows.title: Confidence Routing Calibration + Canary Execute Band

rows.acceptance: Introduce cap-aware scheduling that defers excess runnable work to a parked queue without consuming attempts, with deterministic rehydration when caps reopen and queue-aging SLO receipts. Verify with queue-drain simulation receipts; success criteria: reduce cap-related holds by >=60% and stale-pending backlog by >=30% over 30 days. Rollback by restoring baseline scheduler cap-handling policy.
rows.problem: Daily/canary cap holds are being counted as policy holds instead of being deferred cleanly, inflating hold rate and wasting attempt slots.
rows.title: Cap-Aware Deferred Queue Scheduler

rows.acceptance: Add a preflight routeability lint that classifies proposals as executable/defer/manual before admission; auto-route manual items to human queue and emit structured reasons without attempt burn. Verify with routeability replay receipts and manual-queue routing checks; success criteria: reduce route-block holds by >=70% with zero increase in unsafe executions. Rollback by bypassing preflight lint and restoring legacy admission routing.
rows.problem: `init_gate_blocked_route` events from `not_executable` and manual-gate requirements are entering execution flow too late and turning into avoidable holds.
rows.title: Routeability Preflight Lint (`not_executable` / `gate_manual`)

rows.acceptance: Add token-burst smoothing and budget preflight pacing in the route path, with proactive deferral when projected burn crosses guard bands; emit budget-pressure receipts and enforce rollback-safe bypass policy for emergency-only mode. Success criteria: keep budget-hold rate below 0.05 for 30 consecutive days.
rows.problem: Autopause-triggered budget holds still appear in the window when burn bursts exceed pacing controls, increasing hold churn and forcing hard stops.
rows.title: Budget Burst Smoothing + Autopause Prevention

rows.problem: Launch communication quality was inconsistent and not codified into reusable artifacts.
rows.title: InfRing Launch Announcement Template Pack

rows.acceptance: Human-owned publication evidence is attached via authority artifacts (`HMAN-028`, `HMAN-029`) and corresponding release/post links are recorded in state evidence.
rows.problem: Final publication (GitHub release, npm publish, public posting) requires maintainer account authority and client/legal/commercial approval outside autonomous execution.
rows.title: Public Release + Announcement Publication Authority

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-001` middleware with nonce-bound challenge metadata and per-route policy configuration.
rows.problem: Current payment rails do not expose stateless route-level payment challenge semantics for sovereign micro-access control.
rows.title: HTTP 402 challenge middleware for stateless payment gating

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-002` verification flow with receipt-backed allow/deny outcomes.
rows.problem: Monero-style tx proof authorization path is missing from current payment infrastructure.
rows.title: XMR402 proof verification path

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-003` nonce replay blocking + double-spend mitigation checks with policy receipts.
rows.problem: Stateless payment challenges require strict replay prevention and spend-safety policy controls.
rows.title: Replay and double-spend mitigation guardrails

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-004` approval-gated payment initiation path with spend limits and escalation policies.
rows.problem: Shadows need payment autonomy under covenant limits to unlock sovereign pay-per-resource workflows.
rows.title: Governed autonomous payment initiation for shadows

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-005` config surfaces (RPC endpoint, address strategy, nonce secret policy) with diagnostics.
rows.problem: Protocol viability depends on deterministic RPC/wallet config and address strategy management.
rows.title: RPC/client/runtime/config/subaddress integration contract

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-18-006` optional deep-link metadata emission and stateful payment UX transitions.
rows.problem: Adoption improves with deterministic wallet handoff UX for manual/semi-automatic payment flows.
rows.title: Wallet deep-link + operator payment UX lane

rows.acceptance: Publish approved Monero usage policy + rollout profile before enabling production payment lanes.
rows.problem: Production payment rollout requires explicit client/legal/compliance and operational policy approval for Monero usage.
rows.title: Monero network/compliance profile decision for production rollout

rows.acceptance: Added `protheus lens trigger <pre-sprint|drift-alert|weekly-checkin>` and `protheus lens dashboard [--window=<n>]` in `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` with trigger telemetry (`metric=trigger_activation`) and markdown dashboard summaries tied to `personas/organization/triggers.md`. Verified by `node tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js` (trigger + dashboard assertions). Rollback by removing trigger/dashboard branches and trigger telemetry rows.
rows.problem: Persona consults required manual command memory, which reduced day-to-day adoption and made state visibility fragmented.
rows.title: Workflow Trigger Commands + Persona Dashboard

rows.acceptance: Added deterministic pre-commit gate `client/runtime/systems/personas/pre_commit_lens_gate.ts` (wrapper: `.js`) and wired `.githooks/pre-commit` to run it after staged format/lint. Gate writes receipts under `local/state/personas/pre_commit_lens_gate/` and fail-closes on high-risk staged diff patterns unless explicitly overridden. Verified by `node tests/client-memory-tools/pre_commit_lens_gate.test.js`. Rollback by removing hook invocation and script lane.
rows.problem: Commits could bypass fast local risk screening for security/sovereignty regressions before CI, increasing review load and rollback churn.
rows.title: Pre-Commit Persona Safety Lens Gate

rows.acceptance: Added optional policy-gated feed push path in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/inversion_controller.ts` (`persona_lens_gate.feed_push`) that writes hash-verified `## System Passed` entries to persona feeds and emits feed-push receipts. Default remains off in `client/runtime/config/inversion_policy.json` for behavior-preserving rollout. Verified by `node tests/client-memory-tools/inversion_persona_lens_gate.test.js` with temp persona feed root + receipts checks. Rollback by setting `persona_lens_gate.feed_push.enabled=false` (default) or removing feed-push hook path.
rows.problem: Inversion loop insights were not automatically enriching persona feeds, leaving persona context stale between manual feed updates.
rows.title: Inversion Persona Feed Auto-Push (Policy-Gated)

rows.acceptance: Added `personas/organization/triggers.md` and linked trigger/dashboard operations in `personas/README.md` plus CLI usage surfaces (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts`). Verified through `personas_lens_cli` command-path tests and help-surface coverage in existing CLI suite. Rollback by removing docs references and restoring previous CLI usage text.
rows.problem: Trigger workflows lacked a canonical documented source, reducing repeatability and operator onboarding clarity.
rows.title: Trigger Playbook + Operator Docs Wiring

rows.acceptance: Added native multi-persona CLI path `protheus lens <persona1> <persona2> [personaN...] "<query>" [--expected="..."]` in `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` with divergence-based disagreement detection (`>20%`), deterministic arbitration driven by `personas/organization/arbitration_rules.json`, client/memory/feed recall surfacing, and surprise scoring versus expected baseline. Updated operator help surfaces (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`, `personas/README.md`) and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js` (`multi persona`, conflict detection, arbitration winner, surprise section). Verify via `node tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js` plus command proof `node client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts vikram rohan "Prioritize memory or security first?" --expected="Prioritize memory core determinism first."`; rollback by removing multi-persona branch and restoring single/all persona routing semantics.
rows.problem: Manual persona synthesis created projection bias and reduced scalability for conflict-based reasoning across personas.
rows.title: Multi-Persona Lens Query + Deterministic Arbitration + Surprise/Recall Signal

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/task` exposes deterministic task evaluation and claim-evidence gate behavior with Rust tests and no TS-owned task primitive logic in kernel paths.
rows.problem: Task scheduling authority must be Rust-native to keep kernel portability and deterministic gate enforcement.
rows.title: Task Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/resource` owns quota and allocation checks with deterministic tests; TS budget/task surfaces are wrapper-only.
rows.problem: Resource quota/budget authority in TS introduces drift risk for kernel builds and policy auditability.
rows.title: Resource Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/isolation` owns capability/sandbox gate logic and test coverage for denied/default paths.
rows.problem: Capability isolation must remain fail-closed in Rust for secure substrate swaps.
rows.title: Isolation Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/ipc` owns channel/message envelope validation with deterministic receipt hash behavior and tests.
rows.problem: IPC substrate remained scaffold-level and lacked deterministic Rust message policy checks.
rows.title: IPC Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/storage` owns key/value/version mutation logic with fail-closed key validation and tests.
rows.problem: Storage primitive remained scaffold-level and lacked Rust-native optimistic/versioned state contracts.
rows.title: Storage Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/observability` remains Rust source-of-truth with stable tests, blob loading, and deterministic scoring receipts.
rows.problem: Observability primitive must remain Rust-authoritative for deterministic evidence and resilience scoring.
rows.title: Observability Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `core/layer1/update` owns update package validation and fail-closed policy checks with tests.
rows.problem: Update/install policy at scaffold level blocks auditable kernel-side update safety enforcement.
rows.title: Update Primitive Rust Completion

rows.acceptance: `contract_check` validates `primitive_ts_wrapper_contract` in `client/runtime/config/rust_source_of_truth_policy.json`; wrappers require conduit bridge tokens and forbid legacy bridge imports.
rows.problem: Primitive-adjacent TS lanes can regress into logic ownership unless contract-enforced.
rows.title: Primitive TS Wrapper Contract Enforcement

rows.acceptance: Publish/update primitive residual report with each migration wave, listing non-wrapper TS primitive candidates and target crate mapping.
rows.problem: Remaining primitive-like TS files need explicit residual inventory and burn-down tracking.
rows.title: Primitive Migration Residual Audit

rows.acceptance: Implemented `REQ-14-001` via Rust lane `core/layer0/ops/src/persona_schema_contract.rs` and CLI command `protheus-ops persona-schema-contract <validate|status>` with strict schema-mode validation, deterministic fail-closed receipts, validation metadata, and state artifact persistence.
rows.problem: Structured output support exists but still needs strict fail-closed validation coverage across all persona/lens paths.
rows.title: Strict schema enforcement for persona/lens outputs

rows.acceptance: Implemented `REQ-14-002` via policy contract `client/runtime/config/shadow_budget_governance_policy.json` and Rust runtime lane `core/layer0/ops/src/shadow_budget_governance.rs` exposed as `protheus-ops shadow-budget-governance`. Decisions now enforce per-shadow quotas with burst handling, reserve protection for system-critical capacity, and deterministic receipt telemetry.
rows.problem: Global budgets exist, but fairness and isolation require shadow-level token/compute quota enforcement.
rows.title: Per-shadow budget governance layer

rows.acceptance: Implemented `REQ-14-003` through policy `client/runtime/config/offline_runtime_guard_policy.json` and Rust lane `core/layer0/ops/src/offline_runtime_guard.rs` exposed as `protheus-ops offline-runtime-guard`. Runtime now emits deterministic offline reasons, local-only capability maps, and operator-visible degraded mode messages with receipts/state artifacts.
rows.problem: Offline operation is partially available but inconsistent across research/assimilation workflows.
rows.title: Offline-mode degraded-path hardening

rows.acceptance: Implemented `REQ-14-004` via policy `client/runtime/config/hardware_route_hardening_policy.json` and Rust lane `core/layer0/ops/src/hardware_route_hardening.rs` with CLI command `protheus-ops hardware-route-hardening`. Routing receipts now include resolved profile, preferred/fallback models, context-token limits, and deterministic fallback reasons.
rows.problem: Hardware-aware and local-first routing foundations exist but need explicit runtime policy hardening and receipt transparency.
rows.title: Hardware-aware local routing hardening

rows.acceptance: Migrate memory core hot paths to Rust crate-first architecture with wasm32/iOS/Android targets, preserve thin TS wrapper only, and enforce call-level sovereignty + signed receipts. Verify via device-profile benchmark receipts (`recall <3ms`, memory call p95 `<5ms`, battery impact `<0.8%/24h`) and rollback by feature-flagging Rust memory lane to prior adapter profile. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/memory_hotpath.rs`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_001_memory_core.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.
rows.problem: Memory logic remains split across TS and Rust, limiting deterministic performance, battery control, and share growth toward 50 percent Rust by critical module weight.
rows.title: Memory 100 Percent Rust Core + WASM Mobile Bindings

rows.acceptance: Complete Rust execution runtime + deterministic replay receipt proofs with WASM verification and mobile-safe bindings, retaining TS CLI parse layer only. Verify via 1000-run drift tests + replay parity receipts (`step p95 <5ms`, battery impact `<1.2%/24h`) and rollback by switching runtime selector to prior execution engine. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/execution_replay.rs`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_002_execution_core.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.
rows.problem: Execution state machine and replay paths still rely on mixed-language orchestration that can introduce drift and overhead under high cadence workloads.
rows.title: Execution Deterministic Runtime Rust Cutover + Replay Proofs

rows.acceptance: Move Pinnacle CRDT merge engine fully to Rust with WASM/Tauri mobile background sync hooks and per-merge sovereignty checks. Verify via convergence replay suite + suspend/resume sync receipts (`merge <2ms`, idle battery `<0.5%/24h`) and rollback by reverting to previous merge adapter lane. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/crdt_merge.rs`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_003_crdt_core.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.
rows.problem: Offline merge/conflict resolution remains partially non-Rust, limiting deterministic convergence guarantees on multi-device background sync.
rows.title: Pinnacle CRDT Rust Engine Phone-First

rows.acceptance: Consolidate vault/security compute primitives into shared Rust core with enforced fail-closed covenant behavior and mobile-safe performance constraints. Verify via tamper simulation + crypto policy conformance receipts (`seal <4ms`, background heap growth `0`) and rollback by restoring prior hardened security profiles under emergency policy gate. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/security_vault.rs`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/econ_crypto.rs`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_004_security_vault_core.json`.
rows.problem: Security/vault primitives are not yet fully consolidated under one Rust-first cryptographic runtime with immutable fail-closed behavior.
rows.title: Vault and Security Shared Rust Core (ZK/FHE Envelope)

rows.acceptance: Deliver Rust-first chaos + telemetry core with signed proof artifacts, sovereignty scoring, and low-overhead trace emit path for Tauri/mobile bindings. Verify via chaos throughput + telemetry overhead receipts (`telemetry overhead <1ms`, chaos battery `<3%/24h`) and rollback by toggling back to previous chaos/observability adapters. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/red_chaos.rs`, `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/rust/src/telemetry_emit.rs`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_005_chaos_observability_core.json`.
rows.problem: Chaos and observability hot loops remain fragmented, limiting throughput and increasing runtime overhead on always-on phone and edge deployments.
rows.title: Red Legion Chaos + Observability Rust Merge Core

rows.acceptance: Build Rust mobile adapter layer with one-command WASM/Tauri build, background service integration (iOS/Android), and unified `protheus-mobile` binary interface for crate surfaces. Verify via mobile build matrix + background service receipts with total background battery `<5%/24h`; rollback by disabling mobile adapter lane and reverting to existing device bridges. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/hybrid/mobile/protheus_mobile_adapter.ts`, `client/runtime/config/protheus_mobile_adapter_policy.json`, `local/state/hybrid/mobile_adapter/state.json`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_006_mobile_adapter.json`.
rows.problem: Phone 24x7 objective lacks one production adapter layer exposing all Rust crates through deterministic background-safe APIs and build surfaces.
rows.title: Mobile Adapter Layer (WASM + Tauri iOS/Android Background Service)

rows.acceptance: Add enforced migration gate requiring: critical module-weight share `>=50%` Rust, per-lane committed code diff evidence, test/CI receipts, phone battery receipt bundle, and explicit `PAUSED` state when constraints cannot be met. Verify through governance audit receipts and automated anti-fake completion checks; rollback by downgrading enforcement to advisory mode with expiry and remediation plan. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `client/runtime/config/rust50_migration_program_policy.json`, `docs/client/RUST50_MIGRATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `local/state/ops/rust50_migration_program/rust50_gate_state.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.
rows.problem: Prior Rust plans target 15-25 percent; no hard governance gate currently enforces progression to 50 percent by critical module weight with anti-fake completion evidence.
rows.title: 50 Percent Rust Critical-Weight Enforcement Gate

rows.acceptance: Implement real `core/layer2/execution/` crate (`cdylib` + `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target) that owns deterministic workflow runner, receipt generation, and state machine logic; retain only a thin TS wrapper in `client/runtime/systems/execution/index.ts` through stable FFI. Verify with full parity tests, successful `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release`, full git diff evidence, and tracked line-count delta showing Rust increase and TypeScript decrease (`git ls-files | xargs wc -l` baseline vs after). Rollback by feature-flagged runtime selector restoring previous execution lane and preserving replay compatibility receipts. Completion is blocked until explicit human approval after proof bundle review. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.ts`, `client/runtime/config/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover_policy.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.test.js`, `local/state/ops/rust50_conf001/latest.json`.
rows.problem: Prior execution migration evidence can satisfy weighted governance while still under-delivering public repository language-bar impact and keeping too much logic outside `core/layer2/execution/`.
rows.title: Execution Crate Public-Language-Bar Rust Cutover (No Weighted Tricks)

rows.acceptance: Implement sprint-mode contract requiring: enforcer preamble acknowledgment, single-batch ordered execution plan, immutable no-skip/no-premature-done rule, and end-of-sprint audit artifact summarizing completion, blockers, and proof references. Verify via sprint contract receipts and policy-gate tests; rollback by reverting to existing per-item lane execution flow with advisory-only sprint hints. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_sprint_contract.ts`, `client/runtime/config/rust50_sprint_contract_policy.json`, `tests/client-memory-tools/rust50_sprint_contract.test.js`, `local/state/ops/rust50_sprint_contract/latest.json`.
rows.problem: Repeated micro-prompt execution increases orchestration drift and weakens enforcement traceability for high-stakes migration sprints.
rows.title: Sprint-Mode Enforcer + Batch Audit Contract

rows.acceptance: Execute Sprint 1 strict sequence across four crate-first migrations (`execution`, `pinnacle`, `vault`, `red_legion`), blob integration in all crates, thin TS wrappers, full parity + WASM + battery receipts, and workspace updates. Completion proof must include full `crates/` + `client/runtime/systems/` diffs, successful WASM builds for each migrated crate, and tracked/public Rust language increase evidence with TS decrease. Commit contract: `feat(rust-migration): Sprint 1 - visible Rust increase + 4 core crates + blob integration`. Completion blocked until explicit human audit approval. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/rust50_sprint1_batch.ts`, `client/runtime/config/rust50_sprint1_batch_policy.json`, `local/state/ops/rust50_sprint1_batch/latest.json`.
rows.problem: Existing migration evidence can pass internal gates while under-delivering visible public repository Rust share changes and complete crate-first ownership across critical modules.
rows.title: Sprint 1 Visible Rust Migration Batch (Execution/Pinnacle/Vault/Red Legion)

rows.acceptance: Execute all eleven mega-sprint steps in strict sequence: migrate `execution`, `pinnacle`, `vault`, `red_legion`, `observability`, `graph`, `swarm`, and `mobile` to crate-first Rust ownership; integrate blob fold/unfold + manifest in all eight crates; maintain thin backward-compatible TS wrappers; update root Cargo workspace; and complete full parity + WASM + battery proof suite. Completion requires deterministic artifact bundle: git diff summary for `crates/` and `client/runtime/systems/`, successful `wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` builds for each migrated crate, and public GitHub language-bar evidence showing Rust `>=50%` with TypeScript decrease. Completion is hard-blocked pending explicit human approval after audit. Commit contract: `feat(rust-migration): Mega Sprint 1 - visible 50% Rust + 8 core crates + blob integration`. BLOCKED evidence: `local/state/ops/rust50_mega_sprint1_batch/latest.json` (`rust_share_pct_after=6.253`, `rust_share_min_pct=50`, violation `rust_share_min_ok`).
rows.problem: Public repository language composition remains overwhelmingly TypeScript, and prior internal/weighted migration signals do not guarantee externally visible Rust ownership at the required threshold.
rows.title: Mega Sprint 1 Public 50 Percent Rust Migration (8 Crates + Blobized Core Paths)

rows.acceptance: Implemented fail-closed sandbox execution in `client/runtime/systems/security/execution_sandbox_envelope.ts` with policy-backed workflow/actuation evaluation, blocked command token enforcement, approval-gated high-risk actuation, and deterministic latest/audit receipts. Verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/execution_sandbox_envelope.test.js` and `tests/client-memory-tools/execution_sandbox_rust_wasm_coprocessor_lane.test.js`.
rows.problem: Multi-agent task/file execution needs isolated runtime boundaries and explicit capability control.
rows.title: Secure sandbox execution plane

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-15-002` scoped sub-agent spawn/terminate/report workflow with deterministic aggregation receipts.
rows.problem: Role spawning exists, but project-grade sub-agent lifecycle/termination/reporting needs a formal contract.
rows.title: Dynamic scoped sub-agent spawning

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-15-003` persistent workspace snapshots + reversible memory bridge with audit receipts.
rows.problem: Long-running sandbox tasks need durable plan/progress continuity tied to memory lane governance.
rows.title: Persistent sandbox state bridge

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-15-004` on-demand skill/tool loading with capability boundaries and receipt logs.
rows.problem: Skill ecosystem exists, but sandbox workloads need explicit policy-gated on-demand tool/skill activation.
rows.title: On-demand skill/tool loader for sandbox workloads

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-15-005` staged context loading + token ceiling controls with inspectable compression receipts.
rows.problem: Long task context growth can cause drift/cost spikes without deterministic compression governance.

rows.acceptance: Approve sandbox substrate strategy and publish policy contract before full implementation rollout.
rows.problem: Full sandbox rollout depends on approved runtime substrate across target environments and security policy.

rows.acceptance: Tagged release process produces CycloneDX SBOM + checksum + signed release notes and migration guide (`docs/client/RELEASE_SECURITY_CHECKLIST.md`, `docs/client/releases/v0.2.0_migration_guide.md`, `.github/workflows/release-security-artifacts.yml`).
rows.problem: External trust is capped without a release artifact that includes supply-chain evidence.

rows.title: Public Security Posture Page

rows.acceptance: `.github/dependabot.yml` and `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` exist and are linked in repository badges.
rows.problem: Baseline dependency and static-analysis automation was not enabled by default.
rows.title: Dependabot + CodeQL Baseline

rows.acceptance: Independent audit is commissioned and public report + remediation tracker are published in-repo.
rows.problem: No third-party audit report exists for conduit/policy/receipt surfaces.

rows.acceptance: TLA+/Lean or equivalent proof artifacts cover constitution gate, receipt chain, and conduit command validation invariants with reproducible checks.
rows.problem: Formal proof exists in parts but not as one explicit externally auditable package for core bypass resistance.

rows.acceptance: `SECURITY.md` includes coordinated disclosure process + CVE-ready policy and links starter bounty scope/payouts in `docs/client/BUG_BOUNTY.md`.
rows.problem: Security reporting and bounty process was under-specified for external researchers.
rows.title: CVE-Ready Disclosure + Bug Bounty Policy

rows.acceptance: Launch and track 10+ active external deployments with periodic public posture metrics and incident transparency receipts.
rows.problem: Trust history is weak without real external deployments and transparent reliability/security stats.

rows.acceptance: Scheduled workflow (`.github/workflows/nightly-fuzz-chaos.yml`) is active and produces regular fuzz/chaos evidence reports with triage policy.

rows.problem: High-assurance adoption path is not packaged for air-gapped or regulated environments.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-001` scheduled/ad-hoc conclave flow with participant-scoped position/counterpoint/synthesis receipts.
rows.problem: Multi-persona synthesis currently lacks a formal conclave execution contract with deterministic outputs.
rows.title: Structured shadow conclave runtime lane

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-002` deterministic conclave workspace/transcript contract with regeneration safety.
rows.problem: Long-running persona deliberation needs durable, replayable transcripts beyond one-shot runs.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-003` governed classifier + route map with confidence/reason receipts.
rows.problem: External-eye signals are not yet auto-classified/routed to the most relevant shadows.
rows.title: Eyes signal classifier and routing map lane

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-004` ack/retry/idempotent dispatch bus and operator escalation path.
rows.problem: Routed signals need guaranteed delivery semantics with retries and deterministic escalation.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-005` shadow-consumption -> proposal -> Core-5 review bridge with source traceability.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-22-006` readiness gates with fail-closed/manual fallback and status receipts.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-001` crate with typed envelope (`id/reply_to/route/payload/priority/status`) and baseline unit tests.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-002` and `REQ-12-003` with collision-safe ID generation and lifecycle state map (`pending/in_progress/complete/failed`).

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-004` with policy-backed reroute/retry outcomes and receipt logging.
rows.problem: Swarm resiliency requires deterministic retry/reassign behavior rather than manual intervention loops.
rows.title: Self-healing reroute policy for failed tasks

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-005` with queue-pressure scaling recommendations and reversible actions.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-006` and `REQ-12-007` with schema versioning + deterministic tie-break ordering.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-12-008` and `REQ-12-009` with swarm routing receipts, metrics surface, and atomic upgrade rollback path.
rows.problem: Router operations need auditable traces and safe upgrade/rollback controls to avoid orchestration drift.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-17-001` with context normalization, tool selection receipts, and policy-scoped invocation.
rows.problem: External tool reliability and precision improve with explicit context routing and deterministic tool selection.

rows.acceptance: Implemented bounded external-action egress controls in `client/runtime/lib/egress_gateway.ts` and `client/runtime/systems/security/egress_gateway.ts` with scope/method/domain allowlists, runtime allowlist requirements, per-scope/global rate caps, and persisted local/state/audit receipts. Verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/egress_gateway.test.js` and `tests/client-memory-tools/egress_gateway_lane.test.js`.
rows.problem: Tool integrations require bounded execution to minimize blast radius under autonomy.
rows.title: Sandboxed tool execution for external actions

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-17-003` governed notification channel integration with retry/escalation policy.
rows.problem: Heartbeat/breaker workflows need an operator-facing notification surface for timely intervention.

rows.acceptance: Implement `REQ-17-004` end-to-end shadow/persona bridge with escalation and tests.
rows.problem: Orchestration should invoke tools/notifications through one governed path instead of ad hoc integrations.
